title: Scene In France - Loutherbourg. (Exhibition, 1807.)
content:
Artist, I own thy genius; but the touch
    May be too restless, and the glare too much:
    And sure none ever saw a landscape shine,
    Basking in beams of such a sun as thine,
    But felt a fervid dew upon his phiz,
    And panting cried, O Lord, how hot it is!
meaning:
"Scene In France - Loutherbourg. (Exhibition, 1807.)" is a quintessential example of William Lisle Bowles's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
